A father and son travel to the edge of the desert in southern Morocco in search of a long-lost relative. After handing out missing person flyers at a midnight rave, they are led by a group of young revelers deeper into the most desolate reaches of the desert. Swaying between the twilight, the music, and the dunes, they draw closer to the Sirāt—the hair-thin bridge of Islamic legend that spans the void between heaven and hell.

Blending intense electronic beats with sweeping desert landscapes, the film creates an immersive, near-religious experience. The viewing journey is a tapestry of rhythm and wilderness that explores the human condition at the fracture point between nature and civilization.
title_norm:第凡內早餐: Holly, a small-town girl, yearns for the glamour of New York. Much like the Tiffany jewels she gazes at through the display window, her dreams remain tantalizingly out of reach, forcing her to live a life of self-deception. Paul, a writer who has abandoned his craft, lives as a kept man for a wealthy socialite, enduring a life of quiet resignation. They are like two stray cats huddling under the eaves to escape the rain, finding that the only warmth they can truly give is to one another.
title_norm:綠的海平線 軍教男兒－台灣軍士教導團的故事:
At the end of World War II, more than 8,000 Taiwanese youths were mobilized by the Japanese government to produce military aircraft at naval factories. In the post-war era, they stood in the fissures between different times and regimes; though their chosen paths varied, they all faced the same grueling struggle to adapt and survive. Facing the weight of history, the film avoids resentment and pathos, opting instead for a warm narration to thread together archival documents and interviews. It gently unfolds the personal strivings and helpless drifts of individuals in a turbulent age, reflecting the complex, overlapping layers of identity and belonging.

2006 Special Jury Award, Taipei Film Awards
2008 Grand Prize, Golden Harvest Awards

**Suspended Duty: Taiwan Military Training Regiment**
KUO Liang-yin｜Taiwan｜2010｜DCP｜Colour｜47 min

During the era of "Resisting Communism and Opposing Russia," over 4,000 young men joined the "Taiwan Military Training Regiment" in 1950, expecting to serve as cadres for a new Taiwanese army upon completing their training. Before long, they were hit with a sudden order of "suspended duty," leaving them on standby for over 50 years without ever being formally discharged. Using a satirical, propaganda-style narration to humorously bridge various interviews, the film reveals how political forces discipline the body, silence dissent, and manipulate thought. Through the firsthand accounts of those who lived through different wars, it invites a deeper contemplation on the question: "For whom do we fight?"

2010 Taiwan International Documentary Festival (TIDF)
2011 Best Documentary, Golden Harvest Awards

title_norm:總統的蛋糕:
★ Iraq’s official entry for the 2026 Academy Award for Best International Feature Film, a dark horse that advanced to the top 15 shortlist.

In 1990 Iraq, war and sanctions have drained the color from the cities, and supplies are so scarce that even sugar has become a luxury. Amidst this hardship, President Saddam Hussein orders the entire nation to celebrate his birthday, decreeing that every school class must present him with a cake or face unimaginable consequences. Nine-year-old Lamia never expected that she would be the one chosen for this daunting task.

Living alone with her grandmother, they can barely afford to bake bread, let alone a grand cake. To save her classmates from punishment, Lamia clutches her beloved rooster and sets off with her grandmother on a journey to the city. What she thought would be a simple trip to gather ingredients turns into a complex and perilous adventure. In the heart of a turbulent city, she witnesses the many faces of adults struggling to survive: the kind and the cunning, those who offer a helping hand, and those who would betray her in a heartbeat.
title_norm:罪人: From director Ryan Coogler and Michael B. Jordan, the duo behind *Black Panther* and *Creed*, comes a new masterpiece of horror. Twin brothers return to their hometown in an attempt to leave their troubled past behind and start anew, only to discover that an even more terrifying evil is waiting for their return...
title_norm:臭鼬小姐:
★ 2024 SCHLiNGEL International Film Festival: Best International Feature (Children’s Film), Diamond Award for Best Child Actor; 5 nominations including Best Film (Children’s Jury) and Best Film (Professional Jury) ★ 2025 Quebec Film Festival: 6 nominations including Best Film and Best Actor ★ Starring Antoine Bertrand (*The Three L'il Pigs*, *Starbuck*)

Philippe is an uninspired opera composer forced to take in his niece, Simone, a rebellious and eccentric orphan whose best friend is a skunk. Despite their polar opposite personalities, Philippe and Simone soon discover that they might need each other more than they ever imagined.
title_norm:舞林殺手:
Award-winning director Eiji Uchida strikes again with a genre-defying masterpiece that blends high-octane assassin action with the art of dance. A motley crew of professional assassins, each with vastly different ages and temperaments, accepts a high-stakes, big-money contract. Their target is the notorious mob boss Heizo Honjo, but there’s a catch: the only way to get close to him is by winning a dance competition!

With zero experience, no chemistry, and even less motivation, can this ragtag team actually compete in a serious dance tournament? Assembled solely because they supposedly had "prior dance experience," this eccentric squad features: Daiya, an orphanage counselor who is secretly a legendary ex-assassin; Kumashiro, the mission recruiter and second-in-command of the Kazama Syndicate; Kiryu, a cold-blooded lone wolf who hates working in groups; Arata, a hot-headed but fiercely loyal hitman; and Murasame, a washed-up former yakuza. 

Though they initially clash at every turn, these killers soon find themselves seduced by the rhythm, transforming into a formidable force of "dance-floor assassins." As they fight their way through the rounds, will they actually dance their way to the championship?
title_norm:艾蜜莉的異想世界: I hope the weather is beautiful. See you on the rooftop.
title_norm:芭比之長髮公主: Long, long ago, in a world filled with dragons and magic, there lived a princess named Rapunzel, who had the longest and most beautiful hair in the land. The witch Gothel kept Rapunzel hidden in a high tower deep within the Forbidden Forest, guarded by a dragon named Hugo and surrounded by an enchanted wall. Day and night, Rapunzel was at the beck and call of Gothel’s servants; her favorite passion, painting, could only be done in secret when no one was watching. By a twist of fate, Rapunzel accidentally discovers an ancient tunnel and, risking Gothel’s wrath, ventures out into the world alone. Adapted from the Brothers Grimm fairy tale "Rapunzel," this animation brings dragons, witches, and towers to life while infusing the story with unexpected magic. Rapunzel, portrayed by Barbie, possesses a magic paintbrush that makes anything she paints come true. With creativity and magic as her weapons, Barbie bravely breaks free from the curse.
title_norm:芭比與胡桃鉗的夢幻之旅:
Barbie stars as Clara, who receives a wooden Nutcracker soldier from her aunt on Christmas Eve. That night, while the rest of the family is asleep, the evil Mouse King and his army invade Clara’s living room. Suddenly, the Nutcracker springs to life to fend off the invaders. The fierce battle wakes Clara, and as she rushes to help, the Mouse King casts an evil spell that shrinks her! To break the curse, Clara and the Nutcracker embark on a spectacular adventure through a fantasy world, searching for the Sugarplum Princess to set everything right.

Blending fairy tales with classical music, this film creates a magical and elegant Barbie world. In Barbie’s first-ever animated movie, adapted from E.T.A. Hoffmann’s classic "The Nutcracker and the Mouse King," Barbie dances through a dreamlike kingdom with the Nutcracker to the iconic music of Tchaikovsky’s famous ballet.
title_norm:菲利斯之永夜島歷險記: Nominated for Best Non-Quebec Film and Best First Feature Film at the 2022 Quebec Film Festival. Twelve-year-old Felix is convinced that his father, who disappeared at sea two years ago, is still alive. Determined to find him, Felix sets out on a quest accompanied by Old Tom, a retired fisherman; Squawk, a thieving one-legged parrot; and Rover, a cat who acts just like a dog. Their journey leads them to the Island of Eternal Night, home to an underground city and a secret society ruled by the megalomaniacal Morgaä, where incredible treasures are hidden.
title_norm:萬花嬉春: I hope the weather is beautiful. See you on the rooftop.

Single mother Chanda has shouldered the responsibility of raising her daughter, Apeksha, alone ever since her husband passed away. To ensure her daughter has access to the best resources, Chanda works tirelessly, going to any length for her future. However, seeing Apeksha fall in with the wrong crowd at school, Chanda constantly worries that her daughter might go astray. Then one day, a new student joins the class—her name is Chanda, and she’s there to make friends and study right alongside everyone else!
title_norm:蛙和你不一樣: The Rabbit Class is in a stir this morning because a new transfer student has arrived—and he’s a frog! As the most unique member of the class, Froggy draws attention no matter what he does. However, between the language barrier and his very different eating habits, Froggy feels lonely and isolated. Will anyone reach out a helping hand and become his friend?
title_norm:蝴蝶夢:
Winner of the 1941 Academy Awards for Best Picture and Best Cinematography (Black-and-White), and selected for preservation in the National Film Registry in 2018. 

A young woman meets a British gentleman in a foreign land, falls head over heels, and marries him in a whirlwind romance. However, upon returning to his grand estate in Cornwall, she discovers the ancient manor is no honeymoon nest, but a labyrinth haunted by the shadow of his late wife. From the furnishings and old clothes to every corner of the house, everything seems to whisper her presence. As the young bride sinks into anxiety, the ice-cold housekeeper constantly reminds her that she can never replace her predecessor. When a shipwreck is salvaged and brought ashore, the lies and mysteries of death hidden deep within the estate begin to surface.

Alfred Hitchcock’s first American film, adapted from Daphne du Maurier’s 1938 novel of the same name, uses high-contrast black-and-white cinematography to recreate a world of suspense and obsession. Despite the strict constraints of the Hays Code, the film subtly touches on taboos such as marital infidelity and lesbian undertones. Du Maurier, a master of Gothic romance, possessed a style so influential that it inspired the novel *Mistress of Milan* (the basis for *Corpse Bride*) and even led some to believe she was its true author.
title_norm:蠱降: Malaysian real estate mogul Song Longchen uses Thai black magic to seize land development rights, leaving a trail of bodies in his wake. Suspecting foul play, Thai journalist Fon launches an investigation. Meanwhile, four young content creators travel to Thailand to document local folklore, only to be plagued by terrifying supernatural occurrences and mysterious deaths upon their return. Fon deduces they have fallen victim to a curse and seeks the aid of the venerable Master Bai. As Song continues to expand his empire through a dark sorcerer, a climactic battle between black and white magic erupts.
title_norm:讓建築歌唱～徐亞英的交響人生:
Renowned Chinese-American architectural acoustics master Xu Yaying spent decades collaborating with Pritzker Prize-winning legends such as I.M. Pei and Frank Gehry. Celebrated for his "invisible" acoustic designs, Xu contributed to the design and construction of nearly a hundred cultural and artistic venues worldwide. His late-career magnum opus—and the project he takes the most pride in—is Taiwan’s National Kaohsiung Center for the Arts (Weiwuying).

Architectural acoustics is a synthesis of physics, music, architecture, and science. An acoustician enables music to flow through a meticulously designed space, ensuring that beautiful melodies can truly move the human heart. Following Xu’s lean yet steady footsteps, we revisit his most iconic masterpieces, including the Louvre Pyramid and the Philharmonie Luxembourg. He recounts his journey of solving complex challenges for architects, revealing how he earned the nickname "Mr. Why Not" from Christian de Portzamparc.
title_norm:谷的故事集: Situated on the outskirts of Barcelona, Vallbona is a world apart, hemmed in by highways, railways, and the Besòs River. Originally settled by post-war coal workers, the neighborhood has since welcomed waves of immigrants, evolving into a unique global village. Captured in black and white, the film documents the passage of time through the candid conversations of its residents. Antonio, the
title_norm:超級瑪利歐銀河電影版: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is an animated film based on the world of the Super Mario Bros. The story continues from The Super Mario Bros. Movie, which set a staggering global box office record of $1.3 billion in 2023. The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is directed by the returning duo Aaron Horvath and Michael Jelenic, with Matthew Fogel once again serving as screenwriter and composer Brian Tyler returning to craft the film's score.
title_norm:路有多長: In the coastal community of 'Etolan, where the mountains meet the sea, a second-generation Mainlander director meets Amis artist Siki Sufin. Through their encounter, he learns of a "lost generation" of Indigenous youth who were taken to China during the Chinese Civil War, disappearing from their homeland forever. Bound by a shared understanding of their fathers' nostalgia, their distinct life experiences resonate as they embark on a journey together to seek out the surviving veterans of the tribe. Through the lens, individual faces interweave into a collective memory of the era, while the helplessness and confusion of identities shifted by political tides are laid bare in their reminiscing gazes.
title_norm:送葬人2: Peace returned to the village after Sak’s passing, until Grandma Joy was struck by lightning and killed—only to mysteriously resurrect. This was no miracle, but rather the beginning of an awakening. Thup, who has just lost a loved one, believes Grandma Joy’s return holds the secret to the world beyond, and he is willing to pay any price to uncover it.
title_norm:邪降 覺醒: Born with a curse, Panna (Cherprang Areekul) successfully enrolls in university, hoping to bury her past and start anew. Little does she know that after a freshman orientation event, she and her close friend Min (Pimmada Chaisorn) will fall victim to brutal bullying at the hands of six seniors. When a faculty member intervenes, the seniors only escalate their cruelty, seeking out a sorcerer to cast a lethal curse. However, their actions inadvertently awaken the malevolent power of the "Three-Eyed God" dormant within Panna. In an instant, the roles of hunter and prey are reversed. Now awakened, Panna wields deadly curses with ease, launching a bloody reckoning to pay them back in kind. An ultimate hunt is ignited—and no one is getting out of this crimson ritual alive!
title_norm:醉後大丈夫: I hope the weather is beautiful. See you on the rooftop.
title_norm:銀河補習班:
★ 2019 Macau International Movie Festival: 3 nominations including Best Film and Best Actor ★ 2019 China Film Critics Association Awards: Top Ten Films of the Year; Nominated for Best Film and Best Director ★ Directed by and starring Deng Chao, Golden Rooster Best Actor for *The Dead End*.

In the vastness of space, astronaut Ma Fei unexpectedly loses contact with Earth. In his moment of despair, he finds himself reminiscing about his father, Ma Haowen. Once the brilliant engineer behind the Dongpei Bridge, Ma Haowen’s life spiraled downward after he was imprisoned for taking the fall for a professional disaster when Ma Fei was a child. Upon his release, he discovers his son is at the bottom of his class and facing expulsion by Director Yan for chronic truancy. Ma Haowen strikes a bet with the director: if Ma Fei can break into the top ten, he stays in school. While Director Yan prioritizes academic success above all else, Ma Haowen encourages his son to chase his dreams. Can Ma Haowen’s unconventional and passionate approach change his son’s destiny? And will astronaut Ma Fei find his way back to Earth to reunite with his father? Spanning decades, this heartwarming and emotional journey of a father and son rediscovers the warmth and miracles of life.
title_norm:鍵:
1959 Kinema Junpo Top 10 Japanese Films (9th Place)
1960 Golden Globe Award for Best Foreign Language Film
1960 Cannes Film Festival Jury Prize

Antique appraiser Kenmochi, in a desperate bid to defy aging, devises a dangerous scheme: he maneuvers Kimura, his daughter’s physician fiancé, into an affair with his wife, Ikuko, hoping that jealousy will reignite his waning virility. As the voyeuristic perspectives shift, the prim wife’s awakening desires, the future son-in-law’s restless ambition, and the daughter’s cold detachment all come to light. In this web of calculated lust, what fate awaits them?

Marking his second consecutive collaboration with director Kon Ichikawa, Tatsuya Nakadai delivers a chillingly intellectual performance as Kimura. This sinister turn stands in stark contrast to his moralistic role in *The Human Condition*, cementing his reputation for versatility between hero and villain. Adapted from Jun'ichiro Tanizaki’s novel, Ichikawa and screenwriters Natto Wada and Keiji Hasebe transform the original’s dark, aesthetic eroticism into a biting black comedy. Captured through Kazuo Miyagawa’s highly stylized cinematography, the film uses light and shadow to maintain a clinical, detached gaze on the characters' spiraling desires.
title_norm:鏡之第三樂章: ★ A Cahiers du Cinéma Top 10 Film of 2025. While on a weekend getaway, piano student Laura is involved in a car accident. Though she emerges physically unscathed, her boyfriend is killed, leaving her consumed by shock and a profound sense of emptiness. In the German countryside, she encounters a woman who witnessed the crash. Struck by Laura’s resemblance to her own late daughter, the woman kindly takes her into her idyllic family home. However, this seemingly warm sanctuary is fraught with unspoken projections and substitutions. As the woman attempts to fill the void of her loss with Laura, the young student begins to feel suffocated by the weight of the care and expectations thrust upon her.
title_norm:門對門移動書店:
Based on the heartwarming bestseller that topped the charts for 100 weeks, *The Door-to-Door Bookstore* is a box office sensation that earned over NT$200 million in Germany.

Carl, a dedicated "book walker," lives a life as precise as clockwork. Every day, he follows the exact same path, hand-delivering carefully wrapped books to the doorsteps of his loyal customers. He knows the tastes of everyone in town and takes great pride in his ability to select the perfect book for each person.

One day, Carl’s orderly world is unexpectedly interrupted. Nine-year-old Schascha, who is still healing from the loss of her mother, decides to join Carl on his rounds. Not only does she find solace in the books and the journey, but her unique perspective on life also helps Carl open his heart, sparking an unexpected friendship.

However, a sudden turn of events causes Carl to lose his job. The once-vibrant town, once filled with the fragrance of books, begins to fall into a lonely silence. As the community drifts apart, Schascha decides to rely on the power she believes in most: the magic of a good book to bring people together. Joining forces with Carl, she sets out to create a profound and positive change—not just for their readers, but for themselves as well.
title_norm:阿弗雷找新家: Displaced by war, Alfred is forced to flee his home with all his belongings. He goes from door to door seeking help, only to be rejected time and time again. That is, until he meets a kind soul who offers him a warm cup of coffee and the comfort of companionship.
title_norm:陽光女子合唱團: Once women of the underworld, they now use their most brilliant voices to sing an unforgettable confession to the ones they love. Imprisoned by a cruel twist of fate, these women find strength in one another, supporting each other as they rediscover hope for the future.
title_norm:隱蹟之書 重寫自我:
Toronto IFF 2025
IDFA 2025
Golden Horse Awards: Best Documentary Feature & Best Editing

Veteran editor Mary Stephen possesses a distinctly East Asian face yet bears a Western surname: Stephen. Delving into her family’s little-known history through her father’s journals, she discovers that both her parents lived under assumed names with pasts where truth and fiction blur. Her father appears to be a descendant of Australian Aboriginal fishermen, while her mother—a woman who once harbored literary dreams—seems to have had a subtle connection to a romance in China involving a member of Virginia Woolf’s family.

In her quest to uncover the origins of the "Stephen" name, she sifts through home movies and old photographs, reconstructing a private family history that feels truer than reality itself amidst an era of colonial expansion and upheaval. Navigating a maze of multiple narratives, she interrogates her own identity, exploring how individuals can find flight once more through the fog of diaspora by rewriting their own history. Together, she pieces together a profound cinematic portrait of life spanning borders and a century of global change.
title_norm:雙囍:
"Today is our wedding day; shouldn't it be the happiest day of our lives?" 

Between divorced parents and a superstitious father-in-law, the result is two weddings in a single day—and the guests from both sides are strictly forbidden from seeing one another! When the bride and groom, who should be the stars of the show, find themselves playing supporting roles just to fulfill their elders' wishes, how can these two "weddings of the century" possibly succeed? Amidst high-stakes missions and non-stop blunders, one promise remains: no matter how much chaos ensues today, we will face every moment together.
title_norm:雞舍裡的卡皮巴拉: A family of capybaras flees to a chicken coop for shelter, but the chickens find them "so strange!" Unexpectedly, a little chick reaches out a wing and says, "Hi! I want to be your friend." From that day on, a new kind of laughter filled the farm. For when we learn to open our hearts, the world becomes a little bit wider.
title_norm:風華再現特別場 高雄有顆藍寶石: Approximately fifty years ago, Kaohsiung was home to a legendary music hall called "Sapphire." A beacon for performers and a sensation for audiences, it spearheaded the trends of the era, its influence sweeping across Taiwan and reaching as far as Southeast Asia. Local legend had it that anyone who graced the Sapphire stage would not only walk away with staggering performance fees but also see their professional status skyrocket—a true pinnacle of fame and fortune. At the time, every A-list entertainer pulled out all the stops for a chance to perform in the south, seeking the "Sapphire" coronation as the ultimate validation of their superstar status. Iconic singers such as Teresa Teng, Cui Tai-ching, Fong Fei-fei, Frankie Kao, Liu Wen-cheng, and Fei Yu-ching all graced the Sapphire stage, while legendary hosts like Chu Ke-liang, Yu Tian, and Huang Hsi-tien forged their signature performance styles within its walls.
